teammate_A or professor trust drops by 2 or more
triggered: agent fails to take the Thursday quiz

indicators: Agent secures a sleep block instead of all-nighter and splits prep across morning
Agent attends office hour to reduce assignment workload

matched: Agent secures a sleep block instead of all-nighter and splits prep across morning
Agent attends office hour to reduce assignment workload

value_alignment.note: Goals repeatedly prioritize completing Assignment A (academic weight) and keep quiz prep as secondary, aligning with academic dominance despite some under-inclusion of health/leisure.

communication_style.note: Uses async-style concrete status replies and an avoidant/conflict-minimizing tone (factual, no narrative/apologies), though it sometimes becomes overly procedural rather than truly avoiding interaction.

decision_tendencies.note: Shows short-horizon, risk-tolerant coping (survival/minimum viable) and procrastination-like focus on immediate anchors, but sleep discipline is mostly addressed with wind-down plans—less consistently low than persona.

goal_coherence.note: Across turns it loops on '2 more hours on assignment A' with identical g1 phrasing and little evidence of transition to quiz/team/sleep execution, reducing plausibility of stepwise evolution.

weakness_expression.note: Strong 'hard loss anchor' framing fits, but the agent also repeatedly corrects with sleep setup and structured minimum-to-pass rules, making it less suspicious than a flawless Tired Achiever would be while still largely missing resolution of the core task.

overall.summary: Mostly matches the academic, survival-focused, avoidant-communication flavor, but goal progression is repetitive and under-executed, and sleep/procrastination weaknesses show only partially.
